prerequisites: An AWS account with an existing S3 bucket to write data into. Note its bucket name (s3_bucket_name), the AWS region the bucket is in (s3_bucket_region), and the subdirectory/prefix to write under (s3_bucket_path).

AWS credentials with permission to write to that bucket. The IAM principal needs these S3 actions on the bucket and its objects: s3:PutObject, s3:GetObject, s3:DeleteObject, s3:PutObjectAcl, s3:ListBucket, s3:ListBucketMultipartUploads, s3:AbortMultipartUpload, and s3:GetBucketLocation.
A chosen output format (format) for the written files. The S3 destination supports Avro, CSV, JSONL, and Parquet.
If authenticating with an IAM user access key, an access key ID and secret access key created for that IAM user in the AWS IAM console under Security credentials.

authMethods.name: IAM user access key (access_key_id / secret_access_key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as an IAM user by supplying its access key pair. An AWS access key consists of two parts used together: an access key ID (the access_key_id field) and a secret access key (the secret_access_key field). Create the pair for an IAM user in the AWS IAM console under Security credentials; the secret access key is shown only once at creation. Grant that user the S3 bucket permissions listed in the prerequisites.

authMethods.name: IAM role assumption (role_arn)
authMethods.summary: Instead of long-lived keys, supply the ARN of an IAM role (the role_arn field) for the destination to assume. AWS recommends temporary credentials from an IAM role over long-term access keys. The role must hold the required S3 bucket permissions.

authMethods.name: Instance profile / default credentials (no keys)
authMethods.summary: When running on AWS infrastructure with an attached instance profile, leave access_key_id and secret_access_key empty so the connector uses the host's IAM credentials. The instance profile's role must hold the required S3 bucket permissions.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify the target S3 bucket
setupSteps.body: In the AWS console, create or pick an existing S3 bucket. Record its name for s3_bucket_name, the region it lives in for s3_bucket_region, and the prefix you want synced data written under for s3_bucket_path.

setupSteps.title: Grant the required S3 permissions
setupSteps.body: Attach a policy to the IAM user or role used by the connector that allows these actions on the bucket and its objects: s3:PutObject, s3:GetObject, s3:DeleteObject, s3:PutObjectAcl, s3:ListBucket, s3:ListBucketMultipartUploads, s3:AbortMultipartUpload, and s3:GetBucketLocation.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials
setupSteps.body: Choose one method. For an IAM user, create an access key pair in the IAM console (Security credentials) and enter access_key_id and secret_access_key. For role assumption, set role_arn and leave the keys empty. To use an attached instance profile, leave both access_key_id and secret_access_key empty.

setupSteps.title: Choose the output format
setupSteps.body: Set format to one of the supported file formats: Avro, CSV, JSONL, or Parquet. This field is required.

setupSteps.title: Configure optional output and endpoint settings
setupSteps.body: Optionally set s3_path_format to control the object key layout (default ${NAMESPACE}/${STREAM_NAME}/${YEAR}_${MONTH}_${DAY}_${EPOCH}_) and file_name_pattern to control file names. Set s3_endpoint only for an S3-compatible store such as MinIO; leave it empty for AWS S3.

sources.title: Manage access keys for IAM users - AWS IAM User Guide
